data.content: Here's the conversion. MSTest.Sdk replaces the standard SDK and all three MSTest/test package references:

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to convert to MSTest.Sdk. MSTest.Sdk replaces the standard `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` with `MSTest.Sdk` and eliminates the need for the three MSTest/test SDK package references. The SDK handles all of that automatically.

Key changes:
1. Replace `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` with `MSTest.Sdk/3.3.1` (or similar recent version)
2. Remove the three package references: `MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`
3. `IsPackable` is already set to `false` by default with MSTest.Sdk, so it can be removed


4. Now I'll write out the updated project file with these changes applied.

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
What changed and why:

- **SDK** → `MSTest.Sdk/3.7.3` — this single SDK automatically brings in `MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, so all three `PackageReference` entries are removed.
- **`IsPackable`** removed — `MSTest.Sdk` sets this to `false` by default.
- **Version pinned in SDK attribute** (`3.7.3`) — keeps builds reproducible. You can also manage it via `global.json` if you prefer.

Let me verify it builds:
